pubmed_links: [Uhl et al. (2019) PTPRD: neurobiology, genetics, and initial pharmacology of a pleiotropic contributor to brain phenotypes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30648269/); [Choucair et al. (2015) Evidence that homozygous PTPRD gene microdeletion causes trigonocephaly, hearing loss, and intellectual disability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26082802/); [Cortés et al. (2024) Loss of PTPRD increases the number of cortical neurons, impairs synaptic function and induces autistic-like behaviors in adult mice](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38890753/)
summary: PTPRD encodes a receptor-type protein tyrosine phosphatase that acts as a neuronal cell-adhesion molecule and synaptic organizer; de novo and inherited variants have been identified in autism spectrum disorder, intellectual disability, obsessive-compulsive disorder, and schizophrenia probands, with mouse models recapitulating autistic-like social and synaptic phenotypes. (emerging evidence)
